title: TRIBUTYRIN
description: Tributyrin is a triglyceride containing three molecules of butyrate, acting as a high-bioavailability prodrug of butyric acid. It functions as a histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitor and a primary energy source for colonocytes, widely used to support intestinal barrier integrity, modulate inflammation, and promote systemic metabolic health.

research.title: Tributyrin administration improves intestinal development and health in pre-weaned dairy calves fed milk replacer
research.summary: This study found that tributyrin improves intestinal immune and barrier functions while positively modulating gut microbial composition.

research.title: Dietary tributyrin, an HDAC inhibitor, promotes muscle growth through enhanced terminal differentiation of satellite cells
research.summary: Research indicates tributyrin enhances the activity of muscle stem cells, leading to a significant increase in DNA:protein ratios and overall muscle growth efficiency.

research.title: Evaluation of tributyrin supplementation in milk replacer on diarrhoea occurrence in preweaning Holstein calves
research.summary: Tributyrin supplementation was shown to significantly reduce the frequency and severity of neonatal diarrhea by strengthening the intestinal antioxidant barrier.

research.title: Dietary Tributyrin Attenuates Intestinal Inflammation, Enhances Mitochondrial Function, and Induces Mitophagy in Piglets Challenged with Diquat
research.summary: Tributyrin reduced markers of inflammation (TNF-α, IL-6) and improved mitochondrial function and antioxidant capacity under oxidative stress.

research.title: Prophylactic tributyrin treatment mitigates chronic-binge ethanol-induced intestinal barrier and liver injury
research.summary: Experimental evidence suggests tributyrin protects the gut lining and liver from damage caused by acute or chronic alcohol consumption.

research.title: Maternal tributyrin supplementation in late pregnancy and lactation improves offspring immunity, gut microbiota, and diarrhea rate in a sow model
research.summary: Maternal supplementation improved the immune status and gut health of offspring, highlighting its systemic impact on microbiome development.

sentiment.summary: Users and researchers are highly optimistic about tributyrin's potential as a 'biohack' for lifespan extension and neurological 'dekindling' via HDAC inhibition, though it is primarily recognized for its potent gut-healing properties.

effects.label: Superior Bioavailability:
effects.description: Unlike liquid butyrate which has a foul odor and low reach, tributyrin safely reaches the hindgut for maximum efficacy.

effects.label: Anti-Inflammatory:
effects.description: Consistently reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines like TNF-α and IL-6 in multiple animal models.

effects.label: Growth Promotion:
effects.description: Demonstrates clear improvements in average daily gain and feed efficiency in developing organisms.

effects.label: Alcohol Protection:
effects.description: Effectively mitigates intestinal and liver injury induced by ethanol consumption.

dosage.label: General Gut Support:
dosage.description: Research doses in large animal models often use 0.2% to 0.3% of total caloric intake.

dosage.label: High-Dose Therapeutic:
dosage.description: Human cancer trials have explored up to 400 mg/kg, though this is far above standard supplemental levels.

dosage.label: Calf/Livestock Model:
dosage.description: Common experimental doses range from 2g per liter of milk replacer to 8.3g per kg of feed.

dosage.label: Prodrug Advantage:
dosage.description: Because it is a prodrug, users can achieve higher systemic butyrate levels without the digestive distress of pure butyric acid.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Shift:
sideEffects.description: Rapid changes in gut microbiota may cause temporary bloating or changes in bowel habits.

sideEffects.label: Odor:
sideEffects.description: Though less pungent than butyric acid, some concentrated forms can still have a distinct, slightly unpleasant smell.

sideEffects.label: Metabolic Changes:
sideEffects.description: High doses may influence lipid metabolism and gene expression in the liver.

sideEffects.label: Unknown Long-term Human Data:
sideEffects.description: Most current data is derived from animal models (porcine, bovine) or specific clinical cancer trials.

availability.label: Supplement Form:
availability.description: Widely available as a specialized gut health supplement, often labeled as 'Liquid Butyrate' or 'CoreBiome'.

availability.label: Agricultural Roots:
availability.description: Historically used as a livestock feed additive before gaining popularity in the biohacking and longevity communities.
